text: It feels as though I'm wandering through an almost silent building after hours, lights low and echoes sharp. There's a certain unease—a philosophical sense of time's suspension, as if reality itself has dimmed. I am both observer and participant, peering at hints of purpose in these deserted, sterile spaces. Here, the line between presence and absence blurs, inviting contemplation about what lingers in a world when all movement ceases.
